summary: REQ-29 requires removing the authentication/role guard from AdminPanel.tsx so /admin is accessible without any role check or Internet Identity requirement. The diff shows AdminPanel.tsx has been rewritten to directly render AdminLayout with section management, with no authentication guards, role checks, or 'Access Denied' screen. All admin sections (Dashboard, Users, Technicians, Bookings, Services, Reports) are present. The file summary explicitly states 'all authentication and role-based access guards have been removed'. All acceptance criteria appear to be satisfied. One minor observation: an 'Offers' section was added to the panel which was not mentioned in the requirements, and customer-facing screens were also modified contrary to the non-goals.

hallucinated_features.description: An 'OffersManagement' section ('offers' case) was added to the AdminPanel with a placeholder 'Offers management coming soon' screen, which was not part of the listed admin sections in REQ-29 (Dashboard, Users, Technicians, Bookings, Services, Reports).
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-29 specifies the admin sections that should remain functional as Dashboard, Users, Technicians, Bookings, Services, and Reports. The 'Offers' section is an addition not mentioned anywhere in the build request.

hallucinated_features.description: A prominent 'Admin Panel' shortcut button was added to the customer AccountView for users with an admin profile role, allowing navigation to the admin panel from the account tab.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not request any changes to customer screens. Non-goals explicitly state 'Modifying any customer or technician frontend screens' should not occur.

hallucinated_features.description: App.tsx was modified to pass an onSwitchToAdmin callback to CustomerApp for admin panel navigation without a page reload.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not request changes to App.tsx or any navigation wiring for the admin panel shortcut from customer screens.
